title: Local Input Security, Resource Limits, Privacy, and Diagnostics · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved maze trace contains one plausible coordinate, wall, graph, search, event-loop, focus, render, persistence, package, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded local-data threat model and controls and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify untrusted files, dimensions, strings, paths, package resources, environment state, logs, and denial-of-service consequences.
Use bounded data-only formats and reject pickle or executable object reconstruction for untrusted saves.
Validate dimensions, cell counts, edge counts, nesting, string length, numeric ranges, and total work before allocation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Privacy Diagnostics
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-privacy-diagnostics-gm5g1u" that uses case 1959 to bound local inputs, reject executable serialization, constrain resources, and minimize diagnostics. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Privacy Diagnostics into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Privacy Diagnostics, keep Privacy Diagnostics connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-privacy-diagnostics-gm5g1u
# Competency: maze-privacy-diagnostics.
# Case 1959: a corrupted-state recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: resource use must remain below an explicit budget.
# Changed case: replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path.
def evidence_maze_privacy_diagnostics_gm5g1u(admitted_cells: int = 4096, maximum_cells: int = 16384, rejected_documents: int = 7, executable_formats: int = 0, retained_log_days: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_gm5g1u = "gm5g1u-u1g5mg-1959"
    assert evidence_variant_gm5g1u
    evidence_axes_gm5g1u = (
        "gridgm5g1u",
        "topology1959",
        "searchu1g5mg",
        "eventgm5g1u1959",
        "access1959gm5g1u",
        "recoveryu1g5mg1959",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_gm5g1u)) == 6
    if admitted_cells < 1 or maximum_cells < 1:
        return False, admitted_cells, "resource-shape"
    if admitted_cells > maximum_cells:
        return False, admitted_cells, "cell-budget"
    if rejected_documents < 1:
        return False, rejected_documents, "rejection-evidence"
    if executable_formats > 0 or retained_log_days > 30:
        return False, executable_formats, "local-trust"
    assert admitted_cells <= maximum_cells
    return True, admitted_cells, "bounded-local-data"
